citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
- A male Jacobs sheep had significant lameness on all four feet due to severely overgrown hooves. The hooves on all
four limbs had grown to a point where they were curling and twisted, resulting in a clear negative impact on the animal's
health and wellbeing. Overgrown hooves are a source of pain and mechanical obstruction to normal gait. The facility had
not observed the condition prior to inspection.
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation
of all animals to assess their health and well-being. A mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that
timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending
veterinarian. The hooves must be appropriately trimmed and properly maintained to provide adequate health care for this
animal.
Corrected at time of inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
Additional Inspectors:
MICHAEL TYGART, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Direct
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
- A male Jacobs sheep had significant lameness on all four feet due to severely overgrown hooves. The hooves on all 
four limbs had grown to a point where they were curling and twisted, resulting in a clear negative impact on the animal's 
health and wellbeing. Overgrown hooves are a source of pain and mechanical obstruction to normal gait. The facility had 
not observed the condition prior to inspection.  
 
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation 
of all animals to assess their health and well-being. A mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that 
timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending 
veterinarian. The hooves must be appropriately trimmed and properly maintained to provide adequate health care for this 
animal. 
 
Corrected at time of inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
